title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens left Boston College to turn professional, joining the Providence Bruins on an AHL amateur tryout agreement.
Hagens, the 2025 NHL Draft's seventh overall pick, posted 47 points in 34 games for BC last season.
Bruins organization sees Hagens as a key future player, providing him immediate experience with the AHL team.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony, a 21-year-old outfielder, is expected to be an MVP candidate in his first full year with the Red Sox.
Brayan Bello is a key pitcher in the Red Sox rotation after a strong spring training and World Baseball Classic performance.
Marcelo Mayer needs to overcome durability and offensive struggles to become a regular in the Red Sox lineup.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Bradley H. Jones, Jr., Massachusetts House Minority Leader, will not seek reelection, ending a 32-year career in office.
Jones, representing the 20th Middlesex District, has been in office since March 1994 and was elected 17 times.
He has been the Republican Minority Leader since 2002 and cast over 8,180 roll call votes during his tenure.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: ICE agents have not been deployed to Logan Airport in Boston, according to TSA union president Mike Gayzagian.
DHS officers were seen at Logan as part of a TSA security operation, not directly linked to ICE deployment.
ICE agents deployed to 14 other US airports due to increased wait times and security concerns, acknowledged by Massport.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave pending an external investigation, confirmed by Mayor Sam Squailia.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty is currently serving as acting fire chief during Suarez's leave.
Suarez's leave commenced on February 26, 2026, with no additional details disclosed due to personnel privacy.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was seriously injured in a stabbing at Seaport on Sunday night with wounds to his stomach and leg.
Police discovered the victim at 2:44 a.m. outside 60 Seaport Blvd; he was transported to a local hospital.
No suspects or arrests have been made; the Boston Police Department is actively investigating the incident.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's bench trial started in Essex County Superior Court; she waived her right to a jury trial.
Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, is accused of assault after a colleague shot her during a restraining order service.
Assistant DA argues Fitzsimmons tried to shoot officer Patrick Noonan, while defense claims she was suicidal and misused her weapon.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins hosted the Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at a practice session at Warrior Ice Arena.
Blackstone Valley recently won the Rhode Island Division II state title, marking a dramatic end to their championship run.
Senior Colin Dorgan played a pivotal role in the championship, scoring crucial goals despite personal tragedy during the season.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots signed offensive tackle James Hudson after his release from the Giants earlier this month.
Hudson, entering his sixth NFL season, has played 60 games with 19 starts, mostly at right tackle.
He provides depth for the Patriots' offensive line, backing up 35-year-old right tackle Morgan Moses.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens has finished his sophomore season at Boston College and hasn't signed a pro contract with the Bruins yet.
Hagens, a top NHL prospect, recorded 47 points this season and is a finalist for the Hobey Baker Award.
Marco Sturm, Bruins coach, is open to adding young players like Hagens if they meet performance expectations.
